addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the target project for existing components, layout patterns,and tokens that match the design intent. Reuse what the project already has instead of generating new code from sc… [truncated]

content.text: No React. This is a Ruby/Rails app (Gemfile, config.ru). No `.jsx`/`.tsx` files, no React in `package.json`.

content.content:
def next?
  File.basename(__FILE__) == "Gemfile.next"
end

if next?
  gem 'rails', '~> 7.0'
else
  gem 'rails', '~> 6.1'
end

source 'http://rubygems.org'

gem 'actionpack-cloudfront', git: 'git@github.com:customink/actionpack-cloudfront.git', branch: 'master'
# Reduces boot times through caching; required in config/boot.rb
gem 'bootsnap', require: false
gem 'circuitbox'
gem 'coffee-rails'
gem 'dalli'
gem 'design_client', git: 'git@github.com:customink/design_client'
gem 'dotenv-rails'
gem 'inkjs-rails', git: 'git@github.com:customink/inkjs-rails.git'
gem 'is_it_up'
gem 'is_it_working'
gem 'jquery-rails'
gem 'quote_client', git: 'git@github.com:customink/quote_client.git'
gem 'lograge'
gem 'mms_client', git: 'git@github.com:customink/mms_client.git', branch: 'master'
# These two gems are versioned locked because I was seeing a load error in staging where multi_json was not being found
# when using older versions.  Conversely, when moneta was updated to the latest version it appears an incompatibility was introduced
# with style_bitz so locked at this intermediate version.

end

source 'http://rubygems.org'

gem 'actionpack-cloudfront', git: 'git@github.com:customink/actionpack-cloudfront.git', branch: 'master'
# Reduces boot times through caching; required in config/boot.rb
gem 'bootsnap', require: false
gem 'circuitbox'

gem 'dalli'
gem 'design_client', git: 'git@github.com:customink/design_client'

gem 'lograge'
gem 'mms_client', git: 'git@github.com:customink/mms_client.git', branch: 'master'
# These two gems are versioned locked because I was seeing a load error in staging where multi_json was not being found
# when using older versions.  Conversely, when moneta was updated to the latest version it appears an incompatibility was introduced
# with style_bitz so locked at this intermediate version.

content.content:
# Be sure to restart your server when you modify this file.

# Version of your assets, change this if you want to expire all your assets.
Rails.application.config.assets.version = '1.1' # Using SassC.

# Add additional assets to the asset load path.
# Rails.application.config.assets.paths << Emoji.images_path

# Precompile additional assets.
# application.js, application.css, and all non-JS/CSS in app/assets folder are already added.
# Rails.application.config.assets.precompile += %w( search.js )

# Define the assets load path away from the default '/assets'
# Rails.application.config.assets.prefix = '/assets'

# SiteContent
# -----------
Rails.application.config.assets.cache_limit = 100.megabytes

require 'tilt/coffee'
Rails.application.config.assets.configure do |env|
  env.cache = ActiveSupport::Cache.lookup_store(*DalliConfig.settings)
end if DalliConfig.enabled?

# See https://github.com/sass/sassc-ruby/issues/207
Rails.application.config.assets.export_concurrent = false

Rails.application.config.assets.precompile << lambda do |filename, path|
  sass_partial = !!(filename =~ /\/_[-?\w_?]+\.s?css\z/)
  blacklisted = [
    # Sass partials without the proper _prefix convention.
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/rotators/sb-rotator2014},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/sb-buttons.css},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/sb-shipping},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/stacks/sb-art},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/stacks/sb-booster-engage},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/stacks/sb-inkers},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/stacks/sb-iotw},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/stacks/sb-pricing},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/stacks/sb-product-styles},
    %r{site_content/mixins/style-bitz/vouchers/sb-voucher-style1},
    # Ignore StyleBitz sutff. Guide and poor parital name.
    %r{style_bitz_style_guide},
    %r{style_bitz/mmenu},
    # Exclude jquery-rails assets of unused verions.
    %r{jquery.min},
    %r{jquery2.min},
    # Unknown or other gems.
    %r{tipped.js},
    %r{susyone}, # Eager precompile will yank this in an it uses Compass.
    %r{sg_to_sb/variables}
  ].any? { |x| x === filename }
  if sass_partial || blacklisted
    false
  else
    puts "PRECOMPILING: #{filename}" if ENV['DEBUG'] == '1' || ENV['CI']
    true
  end
end
[result-id: r5]
[rerun: b7]

content.text:
**4/10**

Here's why it's not that bad:

**In your favor:**
- Already has `esbuild` in `package.json` — ideal bundler for React islands
- Rails 6.1/7.0 — modern enough
- Sprockets asset pipeline is already set up with custom precompile rules
- No existing JS framework to fight with (jQuery only)

**The friction:**
- Sprockets + esbuild side-by-side needs wiring (no Webpacker/Vite here)
- No `.erb` → React integration layer yet — you'd need a mount point pattern
- `coffee-rails` still in use — signals older JS conventions, team may resist

**The island pattern itself is simple:** drop a `<div id="react-island-foo">` in ERB, bundle a small React entry point with esbuild, hydrate it. esbuild already knows how to handle JSX.

Main work is the plumbing, not the concept.

case "$TERM_PROGRAM" in ghostty) B='com.mitchellh.ghostty';; iTerm.app) B='com.googlecode.iterm2';; WarpTerminal) B='dev.warp.Warp-Stable';; Apple_Terminal) B='com.apple.Terminal';; *) B='';; esac; terminal-notifier -title 'Claude Code' -message 'Claude finished' -sound 'Glass' ${B:+-activate "$B"} 2>/dev/null || true
